title: Beloperone appendiculata
taxonomy: Beloperone appendiculata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the genus Beloperone, which contains about 15 species of shrubs and small trees. The Acanthaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the acanthus or acanthus family. Beloperone appendiculata is often found growing in open woods and along streams.

intro: Beloperone appendiculata, also known as the "Yellow Bells" or "Golden Trumpet", is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
